# Accepted Fuzzy Match Examples
#
# These are pairs where semantic similarity is high AND context preserves meaning.
# Format: source_text | candidate_translation | expected: ACCEPTED
# Similarity threshold: >= 0.85 with context guard

# 1. Minor word order change, same meaning
"Hello world, how are you?" | "Hello world, how are you doing?" | ACCEPTED

# 2. Synonym substitution, same meaning
"The cat sat on the mat." | "The feline sat on the mat." | ACCEPTED

# 3. Contraction expansion
"I don't know." | "I do not know." | ACCEPTED

# 4. Polite form variation (Japanese context)
"ありがとうございます。" | "ありがとう。" | ACCEPTED

# 5. Honorific variation, same referent
"田中さんが来ました。" | "田中様が来られました。" | ACCEPTED

# 6. Tense consistency maintained
"He walks to school." | "He walks to school every day." | ACCEPTED

# 7. Number format variation
"There are 3 apples." | "There are three apples." | ACCEPTED

# 8. Minor punctuation difference
"Wait!" | "Wait." | ACCEPTED

# 9. Demonstrative pronoun variation
"This is my book." | "That is my book." | ACCEPTED

# 10. Passive/active voice, same meaning
"The book was read by her." | "She read the book." | ACCEPTED

# 11. Adverb placement
"He quickly ran." | "He ran quickly." | ACCEPTED

# 12. Article variation (definite/indefinite)
"A dog barked." | "The dog barked." | ACCEPTED

# 13. Preposition variation, same spatial relation
"In the room." | "Inside the room." | ACCEPTED

# 14. Conjunction variation
"He came and left." | "He came, then left." | ACCEPTED

# 15. Relative clause reduction
"The man who is standing there." | "The man standing there." | ACCEPTED

# 16. Japanese: です/ます form variation
"これは本です。" | "これは本だ。" | ACCEPTED

# 17. Japanese: particle variation (wa/ga) with same topic
"私は学生です。" | "私が学生です。" | ACCEPTED

# 18. Japanese: honorific prefix variation
"お茶を飲みます。" | "茶を飲みます。" | ACCEPTED

# 19. Japanese: verb form variation (dictionary vs masu)
"食べる。" | "食べます。" | ACCEPTED

# 20. Japanese: casual vs polite, same content
"行くよ。" | "行きますよ。" | ACCEPTED
